habitat.text: Britain, Clermond Ferrand in France, and other parts of the Continent.

keynotes.text: The fruit stalks of all species are used, their distinctive characteristics are stalks 1 3/4 inch long, very thin and enlarged at one end.

indications.text: Astringent, tonic. Used in bronchial complaints anaemia, and for looseness of bowels, in the form of an infusion or decoction. 1/2 oz. of the stalks to a pint of water. Purchase from Richters Wild Cherry (Prunus virginiana) Dried Bark
